claims.summary: High-volume signal exchange is presence or influence, not necessarily participation or relationship.
claims.disposition: verify the correspondence provenance and response-state model

claims.summary: Directional row-count asymmetry should remain visible without being mistaken for relational truth.
claims.disposition: verify diagnostics report source ownership and do not infer mutuality from counts

claims.summary: The handshake report must not promote reply continuity into mutual acknowledgement.
claims.disposition: apply and test strict address-receipt eligibility

claims.summary: Forced one-to-one parity or greater correspondence priority changes live scheduling and influence.
claims.disposition: require Mike/operator Tier 5 approval and a bounded comparison

claims.summary: A durable direct-address channel is needed even when traffic remains asymmetric.
claims.disposition: verify existing correspondence threads, envelopes, and reply trace
